notes: Official titled 'master of the offices' mentioned by Ammianus Marcellinus, involved in political-military affairs in late Roman Empire.

docs.title: BOOK XXVII
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … of the offices, who sent all kinds of false and confused statements of the condition of the country, so that the emperor, cautious and wary as he plumed himself on being, was long kept in ignorance of the terrible sufferings of the Africans. 3.

docs.title: BOOK XXVIII
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … the emperor had read it, he neither trusted the report of the master of the offices, framed to defend the misconduct of the count, nor, on the other hand, did he place confidence in these men who made a contrary report; but promised a full investigation into the affair, which however was

docs.title: Letter 7
docs.author: Sidonius Apollinaris
docs.excerpt: But though you shine with the splendor of a conscience and a supremely well-ordered style alike, you should by no means shun us, who praise fine writing even if we cannot write what is praiseworthy. 5.
